div.p: They who have tabernacles, have power over those who have not. The doctrine of eternal judgment;1 Acts 2:41.2 Peter preached, Repent, and be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, for the remission of sins,3 &c.; but in Acts 3:19 he says, Repent, and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out when the times of redemption shall come, and he shall send Jesus,4 &c.

Everlasting covenant5 was made between three personages before the organization of this earth, and relates to their dispensation of things to men on the earth; these personages, according to Abraham’s record, are called God the first, the Creator;6 God the second, the Redeemer;7 and God the third, the witness or Testator.8—MSS
div.div: The Twelve Next to the First Presidency
div.p: President Joseph Smith now arriving, proceeded to state to the conference at considerable length, the object of their present meeting, and, in addition to what President Young had stated in the morning, said that the time had come when the Twelve should be called upon to stand in their place next to the First Presidency, and attend to the settling of immigrants and the business of the Church at the stakes, and assist to bear off the kingdom victoriously to the nations, and as they had been faithful, and had borne the burden in the heat of the day,9 that it was right that they should have an opportunity of providing something for themselves and families,10 and at the same time relieve him, so that he might attend to the business of translating.11

Moved, seconded and carried, that the conference approve of the instructions of President Smith in relation to the Twelve, and that they proceed accordingly to attend to the duties of their office. (Aug. 16, 1841.) DHC 4:403.

Sunday, Sept. 5, 1841. I preached to a large congregation at the stand, on the science and practice of medicine, desiring to persuade the Saints to trust in God12 when sick, and not in an arm of flesh,13 and live by faith14 and not by medicine, or poison; and when they were sick, and had called for the Elders to pray15 for them, and they were not healed, to use herbs and mild food.16—DHC 4:414.
